title: Migrate render loop from Canvas2D to WebGL
overview: The Three.js WebGLRenderer and render loop are already in place (scene.ts, renderLoop.ts). The actual migration work is implementing the stubbed draw() methods in ParticleField and WaveField to create and update Three.js objects (Points, Lines) instead of the no-op Canvas 2D stubs, then removing the placeholder icosahedron mesh so geometry systems become the default visual backend.

current_state.webgl_renderer: Already created in scene.ts via THREE.WebGLRenderer
current_state.render_loop: Already calls renderer.render(scene, camera) each frame in renderLoop.ts
current_state.audio_analysis: Fully wired — bass/treble computed and smoothed each frame
current_state.pointer_entropy: Fully wired — pointer disturbance smoothed each frame
current_state.geometry_systems: ParticleField and WaveField have init() working but draw() is a no-op stub
current_state.placeholder: Wireframe icosahedron mesh rotates as the only visible geometry
current_state.canvas_2d: No Canvas 2D rendering exists — stubs are empty, not legacy 2D code

steps.title: Implement ParticleField Three.js rendering
steps.description: Replace the stubbed draw() with a Three.js Points-based renderer. On init(), create a THREE.BufferGeometry with position and color attributes sized to maxParticles, wrap in THREE.Points with a THREE.PointsMaterial, and add to the scene. On draw(), update position buffer from particle state animated by FrameState (bass → macro drift, treble → jitter, pointer → disturbance), update colors from paletteHue + hueOffset, and flag needsUpdate on the buffers.
details: Add Three.js imports: BufferGeometry, Float32BufferAttribute, Points, PointsMaterial, Color
In init(): create BufferGeometry with Float32BufferAttribute for positions (3 floats per particle) and colors (3 floats per particle)
Create PointsMaterial with vertexColors: true, size based on params, sizeAttenuation: true, transparent: true
Store the Points mesh and BufferGeometry as closure state
Add the Points mesh to the scene

In draw(): iterate particles, apply frame-driven animation (bass scales velocity, treble adds jitter to size, pointer disturbance offsets positions), write into position buffer, flag .needsUpdate = true
Map paletteHue + saturation + particle hueOffset to THREE.Color via HSL, write into color buffer
Apply motionAmplitude scaling to all movement
Handle sparkle via trebleEnergy when enableSparkle is true (modulate alpha or size)
steps.tests_to_update: tests/visual/systems/particleField.test.ts — unskip Canvas 2D-dependent tests, rewrite as Three.js assertions (verify Points mesh added to scene, buffer attribute lengths, position updates per frame)

steps.title: Implement WaveField Three.js rendering
steps.description: Replace the stubbed draw() with Three.js Line-based rendering. On init(), create a THREE.BufferGeometry per wave with position attributes for line segments, wrap each in a THREE.Line with a THREE.LineBasicMaterial, and add to scene. On draw(), recompute wave vertex positions using sine functions modulated by bass (amplitude), treble (frequency shimmer), elapsed time, and pointer disturbance, then flag needsUpdate.
details: Add Three.js imports: BufferGeometry, Float32BufferAttribute, Line, LineBasicMaterial, Group, Color
In init(): create a THREE.Group to hold all wave lines, add to scene
For each wave: create BufferGeometry with ~128 vertices across the width, create LineBasicMaterial with color derived from paletteHue + wave hueOffset
Store the Group and per-wave geometries as closure state

In draw(): for each wave, recompute vertex positions: x spans [-aspect, +aspect], y = wave.y + amplitude * sin(frequency * x + phase + elapsed * cadence) modulated by bassEnergy and pointerDisturbance
Apply motionAmplitude scaling to wave movement
If enableShimmer: modulate line opacity or color brightness by trebleEnergy
Flag position attribute .needsUpdate = true for each wave geometry
steps.tests_to_update: tests/visual/systems/waveField.test.ts — unskip Canvas 2D-dependent tests, rewrite as Three.js assertions (verify Line objects in scene, vertex count, position updates)

steps.title: Clean up scene transitions between placeholder and geometry systems
steps.description: When the geometry system initializes, remove the placeholder mesh and its lights from the scene. The placeholder currently serves as the only visible content before async deps resolve — keep it until geometrySystem.init() is called, then remove it so the real geometry takes over.
details: In renderLoop.ts: after the geoInitialized block where geometrySystem.init() is called, remove the placeholder mesh from the scene via scene.remove(deps.placeholderMesh) and dispose its geometry/material
Also remove the placeholder lights (ambient + directional) — either track them in the placeholder return value or add lights as part of the geometry systems instead
Update placeholder.ts to return the lights so they can be cleaned up: { mesh, ambient, directional }
Update LoopDeps to include placeholderLights or a cleanup function
Add appropriate lighting to the geometry systems (ambient light for particles, or let each system manage its own lighting needs)
Ensure the scene still renders a dark background before async deps resolve (already handled by clear color)
steps.tests_to_update: tests/visual/renderLoop.test.ts — update placeholder mesh tests to verify removal after geometry init, add test that placeholder is removed from scene once geometrySystem is initialized

steps.title: Handle geometry system cleanup on mode switch
steps.description: When modeManager switches modes, the outgoing system's Three.js objects must be removed from the scene to avoid stacking. Add cleanup logic so each geometry system removes its meshes from the scene before the next system inits.
details: Add an optional cleanup() or dispose() method to the GeometrySystem interface in types.ts
Implement cleanup() in ParticleField: remove Points from scene, dispose geometry and material
Implement cleanup() in WaveField: remove Group from scene, dispose all wave geometries and materials
In modeManager.ts draw(): before switching modes, call outgoing system's cleanup() if it exists
After switching, call the new system's init() which adds its objects to the scene
steps.tests_to_update: tests/visual/modeManager.test.ts — add tests verifying cleanup is called on mode switch, and that scene children are replaced

steps.title: Add scene lighting for geometry systems
steps.description: Ensure persistent scene lighting exists independent of the placeholder. Particles need ambient light (or emissive material), waves may need no lighting (unlit LineBasicMaterial). Add a minimal ambient light to the scene that persists across mode switches.
details: Option A: Add ambient light in scene.ts so it's always present (simplest)
Option B: Have each geometry system add its own lights in init() and remove in cleanup()
Prefer Option A — a single ambient light in scene.ts keeps things simple
PointsMaterial and LineBasicMaterial are unlit by default, so lighting may only matter if we use MeshStandardMaterial later
If using unlit materials for both systems, ambient light is cosmetic — add it anyway for future extensibility

steps.title: Verify acceptance criteria and run full test suite
steps.description: Validate all acceptance criteria are met and all tests pass.
checks: AC1: Main render path uses Three.js renderer each frame — already true via renderer.render(scene, camera) in renderLoop.ts
AC2: Existing audio analysis values (bass/treble) are still computed — unchanged in renderLoop.ts
AC3: Pointer entropy values are still computed — unchanged in renderLoop.ts
AC4: 2D canvas systems are no longer the default visual backend — geometry systems now render via Three.js Points/Lines, no Canvas 2D code exists
No console errors on normal load
Scene works with and without autoplay
Mute toggle and info overlay still work

risks: Three.js Points/Line objects in jsdom test environment — may need to mock WebGL context or use simplified assertions
Buffer attribute updates per frame could be a performance concern at high particle counts — mitigate by only updating dirty ranges
Mode switch cleanup must be thorough to avoid GPU memory leaks from undisposed geometries/materials
unchanged_systems: Audio pipeline (analyser.ts, player.ts) — no changes needed
Session seed and mappings (sessionSeed.ts, mappings.ts) — no changes needed
